title: Cheek Treatment Design
excerpt: So in terms of treatment design now I'm going back to what I'm trying to achieve in terms of the aesthetic I want the angle the most projected part of the cheek to be here it's natural as you get older for it to move anterior and inferior so it's probably about here if I can add …

segments.text: so in terms of treatment design now I'm going back to what I'm trying to achieve in terms of the aesthetic I want the angle the most projected part of the cheek to be here it's natural as you get older for it to move anterior and inferior so it's probably about here if I can add a little bit more curvature here focusing the cheek that's going to be very beautifying I then need to have that shape blend in laterally and then blend it into the lower the cheek Junction so angle of the cheek I find it easier to palpate this so feeling for the angle is better than drawing lines on the face and trying to predict where it is so that's always my first step and I can feel the greatest projection of the zygomers on this line often parallel with electrocanthus then you can use this line here between the root of the Helix and the Ayla base there's a line that tracks around and

that usually crosses at the same point so that is the point I need to be more projected now once I've done that I know that I'm going to have to also add volume here and then this the sooth muscle which is slightly depleted if I can add volume there it'll create roundness in the whole cheek area and then we'll blend that back with a couple more injections laterally most likely depending on how that goes now I don't I personally prefer having a plan but then the plan changes as you execute because you'll see things evolve and I might do something slightly different but I will have a good reason for each difference each variation I take off this Clan it's worth just pointing out some natural asymmetry this cheek is Fuller
and there's more less work will be required to harmonize it this one has a little bit more of an indentation so that it becomes more apparent as we start to inject

That usually crosses at the same point so that is the point I need to be more projected now once I've done that I know that I'm going to have to also add volume here and then this the sooth muscle which is slightly deple…

transcript: so in terms of treatment design now I'm going back to what I'm trying to achieve in terms of the aesthetic I want the angle the most projected part of the cheek to be here it's natural as you get older for it to move anterior and inferior so it's probably about here if I can add a little bit more curvature here focusing the cheek that's going to be very beautifying I then need to have that shape blend in laterally and then blend it into the lower the cheek Junction so angle of the cheek I find it easier to palpate this so feeling for the angle is better than drawing lines on the face and trying to predict where it is so that's always my first step and I can feel the greatest projection of the zygomers on this line often parallel with electrocanthus then you can use this line here between the root of the Helix and the Ayla base there's a line that tracks around and that usually crosses at the same point so that is the point I need to be more projected now once I've done that I know that I'm going to have to also add volume here and then this the sooth muscle which is slightly depleted if I can add volume there it'll create roundness in the whole cheek area and then we'll blend that back with a couple more injections laterally most likely depending on how that goes now I don't I personally prefer having a plan but then the plan changes as you execute because you'll see things evolve and I might do something slightly different but I will have a good reason for each difference each variation I take off this Clan it's worth just pointing out some natural asymmetry this cheek is Fuller and there's more less work will be required to harmonize it this one has a little bit more of an indentation so that it becomes more apparent as we start to inject
